.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose_statement: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made, ensuring code quality standards are maintained. It specifies checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style enforcement.
.pre-commit-config.yaml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: repos, repo URLs for pre-commit-hooks and ruff-pre-commit, hook IDs including check-yaml, end-of-file-fixer, trailing-whitespace, requirements-txt-fixer, ruff-check, and ruff-format

dbt_project.yml.purpose_statement: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It serves as the central configuration hub that controls how dbt builds and deploys data models.
dbt_project.yml.semantic_evidence: config-version: 2, name: "jaffle_shop", model-paths: ["models"], profile: default, vars: {"dbt_date:time_zone": "America/Los_Angeles"}, seeds: {jaffle_shop: {+schema: raw}}, models: {jaffle_shop: {staging: {+materialized: view}}}

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose_statement: This macro provides a standardized way to convert cent values to dollar amounts across different database platforms, ensuring consistent decimal precision and rounding behavior. It abstracts database-specific syntax for numeric conversion operations.
macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.semantic_evidence: adapter.dispatch('cents_to_dollars') function call, database-specific implementations for postgres, bigquery, and fabric, numeric casting operations with consistent (16,2) precision

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose_statement: This macro defines the naming convention for database schemas in a dbt project, determining where tables and views are created based on resource type, environment, and custom schema specifications. It ensures data objects are organized consistently across different deployment environments.
macros/generate_schema_name.sql.semantic_evidence: The macro contains conditional logic blocks (node.resource_type == 'seed', custom_schema_name is none, target.name == 'prod') that determine schema placement, and explicitly handles different cases for seed files, default schemas, and production vs non-production environments

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose_statement: This model creates a customer-level summary table that enriches customer data with order history metrics including lifetime spend, order frequency, and customer type classification. It serves as a foundational dataset for customer analytics and segmentation.
models/marts/customers.sql.semantic_evidence: Code implements customer_orders_summary CTE with lifetime spend calculations (lifetime_spend_pretax, lifetime_tax_paid, lifetime_spend), order counting logic (count_lifetime_orders), repeat buyer identification (is_repeat_buyer), and customer classification (customer_type CASE statement)

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose_statement: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates lifetime order and spending metrics per customer, supporting customer analytics and segmentation. It also exposes these metrics through semantic models and predefined queries for reporting.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose_statement: This model serves as a pass-through layer that exposes all location data from the staging layer for downstream consumption. It provides a clean interface for accessing standardized location information without additional transformation.
models/marts/locations.sql.semantic_evidence: CTE named 'locations' selects all columns from stg_locations reference, final select exposes all location records

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose_statement: This semantic model defines the analytical structure for location data, establishing how location dimensions and measures should be interpreted and aggregated in business intelligence contexts. It serves as a metadata layer that maps the physical locations table to analytical concepts like tax rates and operational dates.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose_statement: This model generates a time spine table containing a continuous sequence of dates, typically used for time-series analysis and metric calculations in downstream reporting. It creates a foundational date dimension spanning approximately 10 years (3650 days) starting from a base date.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose_statement: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining order, product, and supply cost data to enable detailed analysis of order profitability and item characteristics. It aggregates supply costs per product and enriches order items with product details and ordering timestamps.
models/marts/order_items.sql.semantic_evidence: Joins order_items with orders, products, and aggregated supply costs; calculates supply_cost per product_id; includes product_name, product_price, is_food_item, is_drink_item classifications

models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose_statement: This file defines the data model, tests, semantic definitions, and metrics for order items in a food service business. It enables analysis of revenue, costs, and profitability at the granularity of individual order items.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose_statement: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with item-level summaries and customer ordering patterns. It aggregates order items to calculate costs, subtotals, and item counts while determining food/drink order types and customer order sequence.
models/marts/orders.sql.semantic_evidence: Code constructs: order_items_summary CTE with supply_cost/product_price aggregations, is_food_item/is_drink_item boolean calculations, customer_order_count window function for customer sequencing, and multiple business metrics like order_cost, count_food_items, count_drink_items

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose_statement: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing business logic for calculating order totals, categorizing orders by food/drink items, and defining metrics for order analysis. It serves as the canonical source for order-related business intelligence.
models/marts/orders.yml.semantic_evidence: models.orders with data_tests validating business calculations, unit_tests verifying food/drink boolean logic, semantic_models defining order entities/measures, metrics defining business KPIs like new_customer_orders and large_orders

models/marts/products.sql.purpose_statement: This model creates a final products mart by selecting all data from the staging products table. It serves as a clean, ready-to-use dataset for downstream reporting and analysis on product information.
models/marts/products.sql.semantic_evidence: CTE named 'products' selects from stg_products ref, final select returns all columns from products CTE

models/marts/products.yml.purpose_statement: This semantic model defines the product dimension for analytics, providing a structured representation of product data with entities and dimensions that can be used to contextualize metrics. It serves as a dimensional model for product-related business intelligence reporting.
models/marts/products.yml.semantic_evidence: semantic_models.name: 'products', semantic_models.description: 'Product dimension table', entities.name: 'product' with type 'primary', dimensions including 'product_name', 'product_type', 'product_price' all defined as categorical types

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose_statement: This model creates a supplies mart by selecting all data from the staging supplies table. It serves as a direct pass-through layer that makes supplies data available for downstream consumption in the analytics environment.
models/marts/supplies.sql.semantic_evidence: CTE named 'supplies' selects all columns from stg_supplies reference; final select retrieves all data from supplies CTE

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose_statement: This semantic model defines the structure and metadata for a supplies dimension table that tracks supply and product combinations. It establishes the business context for supply chain data including supply identifiers, product associations, and supply characteristics like perishability.
models/marts/supplies.yml.semantic_evidence: semantic_models.name: supplies, description: 'Supplies dimension table. The grain of the table is one row per supply and product combination', entities.supply.type: primary, dimensions including supply_id, product_id, supply_name, supply_cost, is_perishable_supply

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose_statement: Defines the raw data sources and table schemas for an e-commerce system, specifically configuring metadata for customer, order, item, store, product, and supply data from the Jaffle Shop business.
models/staging/__sources.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: version, sources; source name 'ecom'; schema 'raw'; table definitions with names like 'raw_customers', 'raw_orders', 'raw_products'; descriptive fields like 'loaded_at_field' and 'description'

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming columns to establish consistent naming conventions for downstream consumption. It extracts customer identifiers and names from the e-commerce source system to create a standardized customer dataset.
models/staging/stg_customers.sql.semantic_evidence: source CTE selects from {{ source('ecom', 'raw_customers') }}, renamed CTE maps id to customer_id and name to customer_name

models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned customer data, ensuring each record represents a unique customer with proper identification. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer between raw customer data and final analytical models.
models/staging/stg_customers.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_customers, description field mentioning 'basic cleaning and transformation', columns with customer_id definition, data_tests including not_null and unique constraints

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames store-related fields while extracting date information from timestamps.
models/staging/stg_locations.sql.semantic_evidence: source CTE selects from ecom.raw_stores, renamed CTE performs field mappings (id→location_id, name→location_name), applies tax_rate passthrough, uses dbt.date_trunc to extract opened_date from opened_at timestamp

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation for cleaned location data, ensuring each location has a unique ID and non-null values, while also including unit tests to verify proper date truncation logic for store opening times.
models/staging/stg_locations.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: models, columns, data_tests, unit_tests; column definitions for location_id with not_null/unique constraints; unit test verifying opened_at timestamp truncation to opened_date

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data by renaming and selecting key fields to create a standardized view of order items. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw data ingestion and downstream analytics or modeling.
models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.semantic_evidence: source CTE selects from {{ source('ecom', 'raw_items') }}, renamed CTE performs field renaming (id as order_item_id, sku as product_id), final select outputs transformed data

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for individual food and drink items that comprise customer orders, ensuring data quality through defined constraints and relationships.
models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_order_items, description: Individual food and drink items that make up our orders, data_tests: not_null, unique, relationships

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw data and downstream analytics models.
models/staging/stg_orders.sql.semantic_evidence: cents_to_dollars macro calls for subtotal, tax_paid, and order_total fields; field renaming from raw_orders schema; date_trunc function for timestamp processing

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned order data, ensuring each order has a unique identifier and valid financial calculations. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for order information in the data warehouse.
models/staging/stg_orders.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_orders, data_tests with expression validation, not_null and unique column constraints

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields while adding calculated columns to classify products as food or drink items.
models/staging/stg_products.sql.semantic_evidence: source CTE selects from raw_products, renamed CTE performs field mappings (sku→product_id, name→product_name), applies cents_to_dollars conversion, and creates boolean flags is_food_item and is_drink_item using coalesce expressions

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose_statement: This YAML file defines the structure and validation rules for a staging layer product data model. It specifies the schema for cleaned and transformed product information with uniqueness and non-null constraints on the primary key.
models/staging/stg_products.yml.semantic_evidence: models key containing stg_products definition, description field explaining data purpose, columns array with product_id specification, data_tests array with not_null and unique constraints

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields while adding calculated columns like supply UUID and converted cost values.
models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.semantic_evidence: dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']) as supply_uuid, cents_to_dollars('cost') as supply_cost, field renaming (id as supply_id, sku as product_id), boolean transformation (perishable as is_perishable_supply)

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for supply expense data, ensuring data quality through uniqueness and non-null constraints on supply identifiers.
models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_supplies, description field mentioning 'supply expenses data with basic cleaning and transformation', supply_uuid column with not_null and unique data_tests

Taskfile.yml.purpose_statement: This is a task automation configuration file that orchestrates data generation and loading workflows for analytics projects. It defines commands for setting up virtual environments, installing dependencies, generating synthetic data, and loading it into a database.
Taskfile.yml.semantic_evidence: YAML keys: version, silent, vars, YEARS, DB, tasks, venv, install; task definitions for venv, install, gen, seed, clean, load; variable interpolation {{.YEARS}} and {{.DB}}
